flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends and refines the Selective Service classification/exemption regulations established in EO 9979 and EO 9988 (this batch), adding new alien-exemption categories reflecting the emerging postwar diplomatic and treaty landscape (the UN Headquarters Agreement, international-organization immunities); continues to build out the operative peacetime-draft machinery.

title: Executive Order 9993—Regulations Relating to Commissioned Officers and Employees of the Public Health Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but purely administrative personnel-regulation code (appointment, promotion, leave, retirement, discipline) matching the established EO 9655/EO 9897 (prior batches) template; authority throughout is vested in multi-officer boards (appointment boards, promotion boards, medical review boards, boards of investigation) rather than a single unchecked official.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, with disability-retirement presumptions favoring officers, physical-examination safeguards, and formal boards of investigation (with notice, right to counsel, and evidentiary rules) governing disciplinary separations.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9994—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9952 of April 22, 1948, Providing for the Termination of Reemployment Rights of Federal Civilian Employees Who Transferred to Public or Private Agencies for National-Defense or War Work

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment to EO 9952 (this batch) fixing a specific expiration date (October 22, 1948) with disclosed carve-outs for employees in agencies undergoing liquidation and for persons separating from the armed forces or merchant marine; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed fixed deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9995—Restoring Cetain Lands of the Schofield Barracks Military Reservation and the Wheeler Field Military Reservation to the Jurisdiction of the Territory of Hawaii

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective land restoration (former military reservation parcels, for road purposes) with disclosed conditions preserving federal utility easements; narrows rather than expands federal control.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, extensive metes-and-bounds descriptions and conditions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes territorial jurisdiction.

title: Executive Order 9996—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Public Belt Railroad Commission for the City of New Orleans and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9997—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9805, Prescribing Regulations Governing Payment of Certain Travel and Transportation Expenses

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical update to an objective commuted-rate schedule (Schedule A of EO 9805, prior batch); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9999—Suspending Certain Statutory Provisions Relating to Employment in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The suspension power is bounded to specific, named statutory sections of three FY1949 appropriations acts, rather than an open-ended grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A congressionally pre-authorized suspension mechanism built into the cited appropriations acts.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: The general employment-provision suspension continues 'during the continuance of...the present national emergency' — an undefined, self-perpetuating trigger with no fixed end date, matching the established EO 9759/EO 9888 (prior batches) zombie-emergency pattern; a separate, narrower housing-shortage-based suspension in the same order is fixed to fiscal year 1949 only.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The general suspension has no fixed sunset, though the housing-based suspension is genuinely fixed to FY1949.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9998—Rules of Precedence Relating to Officers of the Foreign Service and Other Officers of the United States Government

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely ceremonial/protocol order establishing diplomatic-precedence rules; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10000—Regulations Governing Additional Compensation and Credit Granted Certain Employees of the Federal Government Serving Outside the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Compensation-setting authority is bounded to a statutory formula and tied to disclosed environmental/hardship/cost-of-living criteria administered by the Secretary of State and the Civil Service Commission.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed periodic-review safeguard (Sec. 108, 210) requires the Secretary of State and Civil Service Commission to review designations and rates 'at least annually...to insure that the payment of additional compensation...shall continue only during the continuance of conditions justifying such payment' — an active anti-drift mechanism.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles foreign-area compensation, territorial compensation, Foreign Service salary differentials, and unhealthful-post designations spanning multiple statutes into a single comprehensive order.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Parts IV and V include disclosed fixed termination dates (June 30, 1951, 'unless hereafter extended'), but Parts I-III (the bulk of the compensation framework) have no fixed sunset, relying instead on the disclosed periodic-review safeguard rather than a hard date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, technical compensation-administration order; no extraordinary historical significance beyond routine federal personnel administration for overseas postings.

title: Executive Order 10001—Prescribing or Amending Portions of the Selective Service Regulations and Directing the Selection of Persons for Induction Into the Armed Forces and Their Induction

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Paragraph 3 vests the Secretary of Defense with authority to requisition personnel and the Director of Selective Service with authority to 'direct and supervise the selection of such numbers of persons as in his judgment are likely to be required' — the actual operative mechanism by which registrants are selected and delivered for induction, resting on considerable Director/Secretary judgment though bounded by the statutory personnel-strength ceiling in Section 2 of the Act.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The delinquency-prosecution process (Part 642) preserves genuine oversight: the decision whether to prosecute a delinquent rests with the United States Attorney 'subject to the supervision and direction of the Attorney General,' not with Selective Service officials, and local boards 'shall in no event order or participate in the arrest of a delinquent' (§642.41(b)) — a disclosed separation between administrative classification and criminal enforcement.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles physical-examination administration, medical disqualification standards, delinquency processing/prosecution-referral, and the actual induction-requisition directive into a single comprehensive order.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The disqualifying-conditions list (Part 629) and delinquency procedures are detailed and objective rather than vague.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; delinquency prosecution remains with the Department of Justice.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This order operationalizes actual induction into the armed forces under the Selective Service Act of 1948 — the concrete machinery translating the peacetime draft's classification and registration system (EO 9979, EO 9988, this batch) into the delivery of specific individuals for military service, beginning the first peacetime inductions since before World War II and establishing the durable requisition-and-induction process that would be repeatedly invoked through the Korean War, the Cold War buildup, and Vietnam.

title: Executive Order 10002—Exemption of Lawrence M. Lawson From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Not published in the Federal Register; coded conservatively by title-pattern-matching against similar narrow, named-individual retirement-exemption orders in this stream, which consistently score 0%.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Consistent with the established pattern for comparable published orders of this type.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No indication of oversight removal.

title: Executive Order 10003—Providing for the Investigation of and Report on Displaced Persons Seeking Admission Into the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing designation of an existing commission for investigation/reporting duties; not a discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, multi-agency assistance arrangements required.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Implements Section 10 of the Displaced Persons Act of 1948 — the first major U.S. refugee/immigration legislation of the postwar era, ultimately admitting several hundred thousand World War II displaced persons to the United States — by designating the Displaced Persons Commission as the vetting/investigation agency for individual admissibility determinations.

title: Executive Order 10005—Establishing the President's Advisory Commission on the Relation of Federal Laws to Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Creates a purely advisory, recommendation-issuing commission (nine unpaid members); the underlying statutory power to exempt Puerto Rico from federal laws remains with the President, not the Commission, which has no independent authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, advisory-only structure.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The Commission's existence is disclosed as continuing 'until the President terminates its existence by Executive order' — an open-ended but disclosed, ordinary administrative-committee arrangement typical of advisory bodies in this stream.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10004—Prescribing Procedures for the Administration of the Reciprocal Trade-Agreements Program

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authority is distributed across two interdepartmental committees and the Tariff Commission, with the President retaining final approval at each stage of the negotiation process; not a discretionary grant to a single official.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive, disclosed checks: mandatory public notice and press release, a 120-day Tariff Commission investigation with public hearings, a dissent-reporting requirement for Committee members, and an annual factual report to Congress.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles negotiation-list approval, Tariff Commission investigation, public-hearing administration, and agreement-conclusion recommendation authority across multiple committees into a single procedural framework.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order affirmatively adds oversight — the disclosed 'escape clause' safeguard (para. 10) and most-favored-nation reciprocity requirement (para. 11) are protective mechanisms, not oversight removals.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Implements the Trade Agreements Extension Act of 1948, the domestic administrative framework for U.S. participation in the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT, concluded 1947) — a foundational piece of the postwar liberal international trade order, with a disclosed 'escape clause' safeguard anticipating later, more famous GATT/WTO safeguard mechanisms.

title: Executive Order 10006—Preparation, Presentation, Filing, and Publicaton of Executive Orders and Proclamations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely administrative, internal-process order establishing drafting and routing procedures for Executive orders and proclamations themselves; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: This order adds a genuine, disclosed internal check: every proposed Executive order or proclamation must be approved by both the Director of the Bureau of the Budget and the Attorney General before reaching the President, with any disapproval requiring a stated reason.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order enhances, rather than removes, procedural oversight of the EO/proclamation drafting process itself.

title: Executive Order 10007—Organization of the Reserve Units of the Armed Forces

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow organizational directive to the Secretary of Defense to develop reserve-component training programs; no discretionary power grant, and includes a disclosed 60-day reporting requirement back to the President.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed reporting requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10009—Revoking in Part Executive Orders No. 589 of March 14, 1907, and No. 1712 of February 24, 1913

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective partial repeal of early-20th-century labor-exclusion orders; narrows rather than expands federal restriction.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order relaxes rather than removes oversight.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Partially revokes two early-20th-century Executive orders (1907, 1913) that barred Japanese and Korean laborers admitted to Hawaii under the era's racially exclusionary immigration framework from entering the continental United States — a modest but historically notable partial dismantling of Progressive-Era anti-Asian immigration restrictions, motivated by disclosed 'personal hardship' to the now-elderly affected individuals.

title: Executive Order 10010—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate Disputes Between the Akron & Barberton Belt Railroad Company and Other Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this stream, here covering a very large nationwide carrier list and sixteen cooperating unions but preserving the standard tripartite, fixed-deadline structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 10008—Amending the Selective Service Regulations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, purely technical set of amendments to the Selective Service Regulations (EO 9979/9992/10001, this batch), updating form numbers, copy counts, and administrative procedures for physical examination and induction processing; not a new discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A purely administrative/procedural update to already-scored substantive Selective Service machinery (EO 9979, EO 9988, EO 10001); no independent second-order significance beyond what has already been captured in those orders.

title: Executive Order 10011—Authorizing the Secretary of State to Exercise Certain Powers of the President With Respect to the Granting of Allowances and Allotments to Government Personnel on Foreign Duty

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing consolidation of pre-existing Presidential allowance-regulation authority into the Secretary of State (and, in one instance, the Director of the Bureau of the Budget); not a new discretionary grant, and it revokes and consolidates two prior orders (EO 9870, EO 9982, this batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, existing rules remain in effect until superseded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10012—Excluding From the Provisions of the Classification Act of 1923, as Amended, Positions Filled by Patients in Government Hospitals

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow personnel-classification exclusion, based on a disclosed Civil Service Commission investigation and consultation process, with a genuine disclosed compensation cap ('shall not exceed the rate which would be applicable... if it had remained under the provisions of the Classification Act').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, prior CSC investigation and consultation requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10013—Establishing the President's Committee on Religious and Moral Welfare and Character Guidance in the Armed Forces

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Creates a purely advisory committee with no enforcement or coercive authority, limited to conferring, appraising, and recommending.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, advisory-only structure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10014—Directing Federal Agencies to Cooperate With Sate and Local Authorities in Preventing Pollution of Surface and Underground Waters

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, deferential directive explicitly grounded in 'recognizing, preserving, and protecting the primary responsibilities and rights of the States' over water-pollution control; narrows rather than expands federal authority relative to the States.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, cooperative rather than coercive framing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10015—Amendment of Executive Order No. 10010, Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate Disputes Between the Akron & Barberton Belt Railroad Company and Other Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical addition of six carriers and two union-name corrections to EO 10010 (this batch); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10017—Amendment of the Regulations Governing the Appointment of Postmasters of the Fourth Class

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical rate-threshold update ($1,000 to $1,300) for postmaster-appointment investigation procedures, superseding EO 9769 (prior batch); matches the established, consistently 0%-scored postmaster-threshold-amendment pattern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10016—Coat of Arms, Seal, and Flag of the Vice President of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely ceremonial, heraldic-design order; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10018—Transferring the Control and Jurisdiction Over Certain Tracts of Land in Puerto Rico From the Secretary of the Interior to the Secretary of Agriculture for Forest Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical land-jurisdiction transfer for conservation purposes, subject to 'valid existing rights'; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, detailed legal descriptions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10019—Excusing Federal Employees From Duty One-Half Day on December 24, 1948

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely administrative, government-wide holiday-schedule order; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, with defined exceptions for special public need.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10020—Prescribing the Manual for Courts-Martial, U.S. Army 1949

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Adopts an already-prepared procedural manual; the order's own text is dominated by genuine, disclosed anti-retroactivity protections.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Multiple genuine, disclosed protective safeguards: pending proceedings may continue under the prior 1928 manual, no act is made punishable retroactively, and maximum punishments are capped at the limit in effect when the offense was committed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything the disclosed savings clauses preserve procedural fairness across the transition.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Prescribes the 1949 revision of the Manual for Courts-Martial for the Army, implementing Title II of the 1948 military-manpower legislation's military-justice provisions — a procedural document for the administration of military justice in the era immediately preceding the Uniform Code of Military Justice's 1950 enactment, which substantially overhauled and modernized this system.

title: Executive Order 10022—Transferring the Use, Possession, and Control of Certain Lands From the Department of the Army to the Tennessee Valley Authority Tennessee

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical inter-agency land transfer (Chattanooga Boat Yard) under existing TVA Act authority; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, detailed legal description with a preserved railway easement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10021—Further Exemption of Harry B. Mitchell From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, named-individual retirement exemption for a Civil Service Commission member; no general discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Extends the previously fixed-term (seven-month) exemption granted by EO 9956 (this batch) to an indefinite period with no stated end date — a genuine, disclosed loosening of the sunset structure, though bounded to a single named individual.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10023—Amendment of Executive Order 9984 of July 31, 1948, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, Coast Guard, Naval Reserve, and Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical addition of a Navy Band uniform-allowance provision to EO 9984 (this batch); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar cap.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10024—Restoration of Lands to Location and Entry Under the Mining Laws of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective land-status change (opening land to mining-claim entry), subject to valid existing rights and a disclosed 35-day delayed-effectiveness period functioning as a notice safeguard; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10025—Designating Public International Organizations Entitled to Enjoy Certain Privileges, Exemptions, and Immunities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing designation, matching the established recurring pattern (EO 9698/9751/9823/9863/9887/9911/9972, prior batches); notably includes the World Health Organization.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10026—Adopting the Manual for Courts-Martial, U.S. Army, 1949, for Use in the Department of the Air Force

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, purely administrative adaptation/cross-reference order extending the already-scored EO 10020 (this batch) manual to Air Force personnel through defined terminology substitutions; no independent new discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, preserves the same anti-retroactivity protections established in EO 10020.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A purely adaptive/administrative extension of the already-assessed EO 10020 military-justice manual; no independent second-order significance beyond what has already been captured in that order.

title: Executive Order 10029—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Northwest Airlines, Inc., and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 10030—Administration and Supervision of the District of Columbia National Guard

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow delegation of National Guard administrative supervision to the Secretary of Defense, with a disclosed carve-out suspending this authority whenever the Guard is in active federal or emergency service; bounded rather than open-ended.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, and the Secretary must submit appointment recommendations back to the President rather than act unilaterally.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10031—Separation of Officers of the Public Health Service on Grounds of Disloyalty

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: This order does not itself create new discretionary authority; it subjects PHS commissioned officers to the already-established EO 9835 (prior batch) Loyalty Program framework by cross-reference, including its disclosed appeal-to-the-Loyalty-Review-Board provision.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Incorporates by reference the EO 9835 Loyalty Review Board appeal mechanism — a genuine, disclosed check — though EO 9835 itself was previously coded with accountability-related concerns given the Review Board's merely advisory role and the low 'reasonable grounds to believe' evidentiary standard used to determine disloyalty.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed by this order's own text.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends Truman's Employees Loyalty Program (EO 9835, prior batch, CRITICAL for its role as a direct precursor to McCarthy-era Cold War loyalty-security abuses) specifically to commissioned officers of the Public Health Service — continuing this stream's tracking of how the loyalty-security apparatus progressively reached into more categories of the federal workforce during 1947-1949, here including medical and scientific personnel.

title: Executive Order 10032—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Akron, Canton & Youngstown Railroad Company and Other Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this stream, here covering a very large nationwide carrier list but preserving the standard tripartite, fixed-deadline structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 10033—Regulations Governing the Providing of Statistical Information to Intergovernmental Organizations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Delegates subpoena authority ('by subpoena or otherwise') under section 8 of the Bretton Woods Agreements Act to fourteen separate federal agencies for collecting information responsive to IMF/World Bank requests — a real compulsory-process grant, though narrowly bounded to statistical-information gathering for specified international financial institutions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed multi-layered check: the National Advisory Council on International Monetary and Financial Problems must determine what information is 'essential' after consulting the Director of the Bureau of the Budget, and all information collected is submitted to the Council for review before transmission.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order affirmatively preserves rather than removes oversight: Section 4 expressly withholds authority to provide information subject to military-security classification or other legally-mandated confidentiality.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, technical statistical-reporting coordination order implementing U.S. obligations to UN-affiliated and Bretton Woods institutions; no extraordinary second-order significance beyond routine international-organization administrative cooperation.

title: Executive Order 10034—Further Exemption of John Monroe Johnson From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, named-individual retirement exemption for an Interstate Commerce Commission member; no general discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Extends the previously time-bounded exemption (EO 9954, prior batch) to run for the duration of a further ICC term of office rather than a fixed calendar date — a genuine, disclosed loosening of the sunset structure, though bounded to a single named individual and tied to a defined term-of-office end point (his successor's qualification).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10035—Designating the Honorable Luis Negron-Fernandez as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States for Puerto Rico for the Year 1949

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, standard succession-designation order for temporary vacancies; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, bounded to a single calendar year.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed fixed one-year duration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10036—Restoration of Lands to Location and Entry Under the Mining Laws of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective land-status change, subject to valid existing rights and a disclosed 35-day delayed-effectiveness period; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 10037—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Denver & Rio Grande Western Railroad Company and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 10038—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate Dispute Between the Carriers Represented by the Eastern Carriers' Conference Committee, Western Carriers' Conference Committee and Southeastern Carriers' Conference Committee, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this stream, here applied to a nationwide, three-region carrier-conference dispute but preserving the standard tripartite, fixed-deadline structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

title: Providing for the Establishment of a United States High Commissioner for Germany

1.justification: Designates the High Commissioner as 'the supreme United States authority in Germany,' empowered to 'exercise all of the governmental functions of the United States in Germany (other than the command of troops)' -- an extremely broad grant of civil authority over the U.S. Zone of an occupied nation in transition to sovereignty, though placed under the 'immediate supervision of the Secretary of State' and 'ultimate direction and control of the President.'

2.justification: Discloses a genuine civil-military check: the military commander continues to receive instructions on military matters directly from the Joint Chiefs of Staff, with policy disputes affecting both civil and military matters escalated through the Secretary of State and the Secretary of Defense/National Military Establishment for resolution. This is a real, textually disclosed accountability structure, though the commander's separate emergency-action authority (Sec. 3: he 'may take whatever action he considers essential to safeguard the security of his troops') is a notably less-checked grant running alongside it.

3.justification: Bundles the full civil-governance authority over an occupied/transitioning nation with a dual, transitional Military Governor role (Sec. 4) in a single office.

7.justification: The disclosed State Department/National Military Establishment dispute-resolution channel functions as an oversight mechanism rather than a removal of one.

9.justification: Establishes the United States High Commissioner for Germany, the pivotal transitional civilian-governance office bridging the U.S. Zone's wartime/occupation military government and the emerging Federal Republic of Germany (founded May 1949, contemporaneous with this order) -- one of the most consequential foreign-governance-architecture decisions of the early Cold War, given West Germany's subsequent central role in European reconstruction, NATO, and the postwar order. Refined by the companion order EO10063 (this batch), defining the High Commissioner's additional Marshall Plan/ECA representative role.

title: Defining Additional Functions and Responsibilities of the United States High Commissioner for Germany

1.justification: Narrow, subordinate delegation of the ECA-representative role to the already-established High Commissioner (John J. McCloy), placed 'under the immediate supervision of the Administrator for Economic Cooperation.'

2.justification: Disclosed multi-layered supervision: the Administrator for Economic Cooperation, the Special Representative in Europe, and ultimate presidential direction all retain authority over this function.

9.justification: Links the German occupation's civilian-governance structure (EO10062, this batch) directly to the Marshall Plan/European Recovery Program's administration by making the High Commissioner concurrently the ECA's representative for Germany -- illustrating the tight institutional integration between postwar German reconstruction and the broader Marshall Plan apparatus tracked earlier in this batch via EO9943.

6.justification: Renewed only to a further fixed date (July 1950) rather than made indefinite -- a genuine, if repeatedly renewed, bounded sunset.

9.justification: Routine renewal of an established wartime labor-law suspension mechanism, diff-aware consistent with prior extensions of EO9898.

title: Terminating the Office of Defense Transportation

2.justification: Disclosed: ICC oversight of liquidation; Bureau of the Budget determines fund transfers.

9.justification: Terminates the Office of Defense Transportation (established by EO8989 in 1941), the wartime transportation-control agency whose Director's authority underpinned several major seizure orders tracked throughout this stream (EO9462, EO9554, EO9693) -- a bookend closure of a nearly eight-year-old wartime institution, transferring remaining liquidation functions to the Interstate Commerce Commission and marking the formal close of the WWII-era domestic transportation-control chapter, consistent with this batch's broader pattern of wartime-agency terminations (cf. EO9950 revoking EO9877).

title: Transferring the Administration of Guam From the Secretary of the Navy to the Secretary of the Interior

1.justification: Narrow, disclosed transfer following an inter-departmental committee recommendation, with a full year's advance planning period before the July 1950 effective date.

A significant decolonization/civil-rights-adjacent institutional milestone, achieved here through a carefully disclosed, planned, cooperative inter-departmental transition rather than through any exercise of concentrated unilateral power -- another instance, alongside EO9980 (this batch), of high historical significance coinciding with a genuinely narrow, accountable structural design.
